title: Understanding the Problem
text: The cyclist's journey can be broken down into three segments: $$5.0$$ km east, $$10.0$$ km $$20$$ degrees west of north, and $$8.0$$ km west. We need to find the resultant displacement vector from the starting point.

title: Breaking Down the Journey
text: Consider each segment of the journey separately and express them as vectors. The first segment is straightforward: $$5.0$$ km east is represented as $$5.0i$$.

title: Second Segment Vector
text: For the second segment, decompose the $$10.0$$ km vector into its north and west components using trigonometry. Use sine and cosine functions with the angle $$20$$ degrees west of north.

title: Calculating the Second Segment
text: Calculate the north and west components of the $$10.0$$ km vector. Use:\nNorth component $$=$$ $$10cos\left(20\right)$$\nWest component $$=$$ $$10sin\left(20\right)$$

title: Third Segment Vector
text: The third segment is $$8.0$$ km west, which can be represented as $$-8.0i$$.

title: Combining the Vectors
text: Add the vectors from each segment to find the total displacement:\n$$5.0i$$ + $$(9.4j-3.4i)$$ + $$(-8.0i)$$

title: Final Displacement
text: The final displacement vector is the sum of all the individual vectors. Simplify the expression to find the resultant vector.
